revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称 = 大山祇神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=神社|cat2=西海市}} |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[西海市]][[大島町]] |祭神 = [[オオヤマツミ|大山積神]] |社格 = |創建 = [[1930年]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 大祭11月3日 |神事 = }} '''大山祇神社'''（おおやまずみじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[西海市]][[大島 (長崎県西海市)|]]にある三島・…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 大山祇神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=神社|cat2=西海市}} |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[西海市]][[大島町]] |祭神 = [[オオヤマツミ|大山積神]] |社格 = |創建 = [[1930年]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 大祭11月3日 |神事 = }} '''大山祇神社'''（おおやまずみじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[西海市]][[大島 (長崎県西海市)|大島]]にある[[三島・大山祇信仰]]の[[神社]]。
==歴史== 大島の大山祇神社は、[[松島炭鉱]]や[[大島炭鉱]]の守護神として、[[愛媛県]][[大三島]]の[[大山祇神社]]から勧請されたことに始まる<ref name=全国鉱山と大山祇神社>[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)]（大山祇神社社務所）</ref>。
その後[[鉱山]]の閉山に伴い、隣の[[蛎浦島]]にある浅間神社（大山積神の子[[コノハナサクヤヒメ|木花之佐久夜毘売]]を奉る）に合祀されたが、企業誘致により[[大島造船所]]が進出すると、住民や大島造船所、三井松島産業により再建された<ref name=長崎新聞/>。大山祇神社の本社も島に位置しており、航海神としても著名である。
* [[1930年]]11月29、[[松島炭鉱]]の守護神として、[[愛媛県]][[大三島]]の[[大山祇神社]]から[[松島]]に勧請、創建された<ref name=全国鉱山と大山祇神社/>。 * [[1936年]]12月6日、炭鉱本部の移転に伴い、西彼大島に遷座<ref name=全国鉱山と大山祇神社/>。 * [[1970年]]、大島炭鉱閉山。[[蛎浦島]]の浅間神社に合祀される<ref name=長崎新聞/>。 * [[1980年]]10月、住民や[[大島造船所]]、[[三井松島ホールディングス|三井松島産業]]により元の場所に再建<ref name=長崎新聞>[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 大崎高野球部員が神輿 炭鉱で栄えた時代 思い起こし]（長崎新聞）</ref>。 * [[2018年]]、現在地に遷座。
[[11月3日]]の大祭では、[[神輿]]や[[浦安の舞]]が奉納される。
== 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

{{神社 |名称 = 大山祇神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=神社|cat2=西海市}} |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[西海市]][[大島町]] |祭神 = [[オオヤマツミ|大山積神]] |社格 = |創建 = [[1930年]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 大祭11月第1日曜 |神事 = }} '''大山祇神社'''（おおやまずみじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[西海市]][[大島 (長崎県西海市)|大島]]にある[[三島・大山祇信仰]]の[[神社]]。
==歴史== 大島の大山祇神社は、[[松島炭鉱]]や[[大島炭鉱]]の守護神として、[[愛媛県]][[大三島]]の[[大山祇神社]]から勧請されたことに始まる<ref name=全国鉱山と大山祇神社>[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)]（大山祇神社社務所）</ref>。
その後[[鉱山]]の閉山に伴い、隣の[[蛎浦島]]にある浅間神社（大山積神の子[[コノハナサクヤヒメ|木花之佐久夜毘売]]を奉る）に合祀されたが、企業誘致により[[大島造船所]]が進出すると、住民や大島造船所、三井松島産業により再建された<ref name=長崎新聞/>。大山祇神社の本社も島に位置しており、航海神としても著名である。
* [[1930年]]11月29、[[松島炭鉱]]の守護神として、[[愛媛県]][[大三島]]の[[大山祇神社]]から[[松島]]に勧請、創建された<ref name=全国鉱山と大山祇神社/>。 * [[1936年]]12月6日、炭鉱本部の移転に伴い、西彼大島に遷座<ref name=全国鉱山と大山祇神社/>。 * [[1970年]]、大島炭鉱閉山。[[蛎浦島]]の浅間神社に合祀される<ref name=長崎新聞/>。 * [[1980年]]10月、住民や[[大島造船所]]、[[三井松島ホールディングス|三井松島産業]]により元の場所に再建<ref name=長崎新聞>[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 大崎高野球部員が神輿 炭鉱で栄えた時代 思い起こし]（長崎新聞）</ref>。 * [[2018年]]、現在地に遷座。
[[11月]]の大祭では、[[神輿]]や[[浦安の舞]]が奉納される。

{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおやまつみしんしや}} [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:西海市の歴史]] [[Category:三島・大山祇信仰]]

{{神社 |名称 = 大山祇神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=神社|cat2=西海市}} |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[西海市]][[大島町]] |祭神 = [[オオヤマツミ|大山積神]] |社格 = |創建 = [[1930年]] |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 大祭11月第1日曜 |神事 = }} '''大山祇神社'''（おおやまづみじんじゃ）は、[[長崎県]][[西海市]][[大島 (長崎県西海市)|大島]]にある[[三島・大山祇信仰]]の[[神社]]。
==祭神== * [[オオヤマツミ|大山積神]] 代表的な山の神で、全国の炭田、金山等に勧請された。別名『和多志大神』（渡しの神、の意）。これは[[大山祇神社]]の本社が島（[[大三島]]）に立地していることから、航海神として位置付けられたもの。このため海神として造船メーカーや海運会社からも崇敬されている。
==歴史== 大島の大山祇神社は、[[松島炭鉱]]や[[大島炭鉱]]の守護神として、[[愛媛県]][[大三島]]の[[大山祇神社]]から勧請されたことに始まる<ref name=全国鉱山と大山祇神社>[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)]（大山祇神社社務所）</ref>。
その後[[鉱山]]の閉山に伴い、隣の[[蛎浦島]]にある浅間神社（大山積神の子[[コノハナサクヤヒメ|木花之佐久夜毘売]]を奉る）に合祀されたが、企業誘致により[[大島造船所]]が進出すると、住民や大島造船所、三井松島産業により再建された<ref name=長崎新聞/>。
* [[1930年]]11月29、[[松島炭鉱]]の守護神として、[[愛媛県]][[大三島]]の[[大山祇神社]]から[[松島]]に勧請、創建された<ref name=全国鉱山と大山祇神社/>。 * [[1936年]]12月6日、炭鉱本部の移転に伴い、西彼大島に遷座<ref name=全国鉱山と大山祇神社/>。 * [[1970年]]、大島炭鉱閉山。[[蛎浦島]]の浅間神社に合祀される<ref name=長崎新聞/>。 * [[1980年]]10月、住民や[[大島造船所]]、[[三井松島ホールディングス|三井松島産業]]により元の場所に再建<ref name=長崎新聞>[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 大崎高野球部員が神輿 炭鉱で栄えた時代 思い起こし]（長崎新聞）</ref>。 * [[2018年]]、現在地に遷座。
[[11月]]の大祭では、[[神輿]]や[[浦安の舞]]が奉納される。

revision.text: == Shrines with Articles == * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishiaizu)|ja|大山祇神社 (西会津町)}} (Nishiaizu, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamatsumi Shrine (Iitate)|ja|山津見神社 (飯舘村)}} (Iitate, Fukushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Yamazu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|山住神社}} (Hamamatsu, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Sado)|ja|大山祇神社 (佐渡市)}} (Sado, Niigata Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine; associated with the [[Sado Gold Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ise)|ja|大山祇神社 (伊勢市)}} (Ise, Mie Prefecture) - A subsidiary shrine within the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] precincts. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nantan)|ja|大山祇神社 (南丹市)}} (Nantan, Kyoto Prefecture) - Not dedicated to Ōyamazumi. * {{ill|Bekkyū Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|別宮大山祇神社}} (Bekkyū, Imabari, Ehime Prefecture) - Former prefectural shrine. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine|ja|大山祇神社 (今治市大三島)|Ōyamazumi Shrine=Ōyamazumi Shrine|lt=Ōyamazumi Shrine (Ōmishima)}} - Head shrine of all Ōyamazumi shrines nationwide. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Nishi no Tani)|ja|大山祇神社 (西の谷)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi Shrine (Niihama)|ja|大山積神社 (新居浜市)}} (Niihama, Ehime Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Besshi Copper Mine]]. * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Dōdoigō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町道土井郷)}} (Dōdoigō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Imazatogō)|ja|大山祇神社 (新上五島町今里郷)}} (Imazatogō, Shinkamigoto, Nagasaki Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. [[category:new pages]][[category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]]

* * {{ill|Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)|ja|大山祇神社 (西海市)}} (Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture) - Associated with the [[Nishisonogi Coalfield]]. [[category:new pages]][[category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]]

* * [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Ōyamazumi Shrine | image = {{画像募集中|cat=Shrines|cat2=Saikai, Nagasaki}} | location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Saikai, Nagasaki|ja|西海市}}, {{ill|Ōshima (Saikai, Nagasaki)|ja|大島}} | deities = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神}} | established= 1930 | main_hall = | alias = | pilgrimage = | annual_festival = First Sunday of November | ceremonies = }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a **Shinto shrine** located on **Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture**. It is part of the **Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)**.
## 祭神 (Enshrined Deities) * **Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)** A major **mountain deity**, widely enshrined at **coal mines and gold mines** across Japan. Also known as **Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)**, meaning "God of Crossing," due to the **location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island**. As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also **worshiped as a sea deity** and is revered by **shipbuilding companies and maritime industries**.
## 歴史 (History) The **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island** was originally founded as a **guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine**. The shrine was established through an **enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was **merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**, where **Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi**, is enshrined. However, when **Ōshima Shipyard** was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings** worked together to **rebuild the shrine**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
### Key Events * **November 29, 1930** – Enshrined from **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima** and established as a **guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **December 6, 1936** – Relocated to **Ōshima Island** following the transfer of the **coal mine headquarters**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **1970** – **Ōshima Coal Mine closed**, and the shrine was merged with **Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/> * **October 1980** – **Rebuilt at the original site** with support from local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref> * **2018** – Relocated to **its current site**.
During the **Grand Festival in November**, **a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)** are performed.

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a **Shinto shrine** located on **Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture**. It is part of the **Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)**.
## 祭神 (Enshrined Deities) * **Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)** A major **mountain deity**, widely enshrined at **coal mines and gold mines** across Japan. Also known as **Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)**, meaning "God of Crossing," due to the **location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island**. As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also **worshiped as a sea deity** and is revered by **shipbuilding companies and maritime industries**.
## 歴史 (History) The **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island** was originally founded as a **guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine**. The shrine was established through an **enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was **merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**, where **Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi**, is enshrined. However, when **Ōshima Shipyard** was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings** worked together to **rebuild the shrine**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
### Key Events * **November 29, 1930** – Enshrined from **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima** and established as a **guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **December 6, 1936** – Relocated to **Ōshima Island** following the transfer of the **coal mine headquarters**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **1970** – **Ōshima Coal Mine closed**, and the shrine was merged with **Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/> * **October 1980** – **Rebuilt at the original site** with support from local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref> * **2018** – Relocated to **its current site**.
During the **Grand Festival in November**, **a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)** are performed.

* * [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Ōyamazumi Shrine | image = {{画像募集中|cat=Shrines|cat2=Saikai, Nagasaki}} | location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Saikai, Nagasaki|ja|西海市}}, {{ill|Ōshima (Saikai, Nagasaki)|ja|大島}} | deities = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神}} | established= 1930 | main_hall = | alias = | pilgrimage = | annual_festival = First Sunday of November | ceremonies = }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a **Shinto shrine** located on **Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture**. It is part of the **Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)**.
## 祭神 (Enshrined Deities) * **Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)** A major **mountain deity**, widely enshrined at **coal mines and gold mines** across Japan. Also known as **Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)**, meaning "God of Crossing," due to the **location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island**. As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also **worshiped as a sea deity** and is revered by **shipbuilding companies and maritime industries**.
## 歴史 (History) The **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island** was originally founded as a **guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine**. The shrine was established through an **enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was **merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**, where **Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi**, is enshrined. However, when **Ōshima Shipyard** was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings** worked together to **rebuild the shrine**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
### Key Events * **November 29, 1930** – Enshrined from **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima** and established as a **guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **December 6, 1936** – Relocated to **Ōshima Island** following the transfer of the **coal mine headquarters**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **1970** – **Ōshima Coal Mine closed**, and the shrine was merged with **Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/> * **October 1980** – **Rebuilt at the original site** with support from local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref> * **2018** – Relocated to **its current site**.
During the **Grand Festival in November**, **a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)** are performed.

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a **Shinto shrine** located on **Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture**. It is part of the **Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)**.
## 祭神 (Enshrined Deities) * **Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)** A major **mountain deity**, widely enshrined at **coal mines and gold mines** across Japan. Also known as **Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)**, meaning "God of Crossing," due to the **location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island**. As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also **worshiped as a sea deity** and is revered by **shipbuilding companies and maritime industries**.
## 歴史 (History) The **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island** was originally founded as a **guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine**. The shrine was established through an **enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was **merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**, where **Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi**, is enshrined. However, when **Ōshima Shipyard** was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings** worked together to **rebuild the shrine**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
### Key Events * **November 29, 1930** – Enshrined from **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima** and established as a **guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **December 6, 1936** – Relocated to **Ōshima Island** following the transfer of the **coal mine headquarters**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **1970** – **Ōshima Coal Mine closed**, and the shrine was merged with **Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/> * **October 1980** – **Rebuilt at the original site** with support from local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref> * **2018** – Relocated to **its current site**.
During the **Grand Festival in November**, **a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)** are performed.

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a **Shinto shrine** located on **Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture**. It is part of the **Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)**.
## 祭神 (Enshrined Deities) * **Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)** A major **mountain deity**, widely enshrined at **coal mines and gold mines** across Japan. Also known as **Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)**, meaning "God of Crossing," due to the **location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island**. As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also **worshiped as a sea deity** and is revered by **shipbuilding companies and maritime industries**.
## 歴史 (History) The **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island** was originally founded as a **guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine**. The shrine was established through an **enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was **merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**, where **Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi**, is enshrined. However, when **Ōshima Shipyard** was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings** worked together to **rebuild the shrine**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>

During the **Grand Festival in November**, **a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)** are performed.

### Key Events * **November 29, 1930** – Enshrined from **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima** and established as a **guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **December 6, 1936** – Relocated to **Ōshima Island** following the transfer of the **coal mine headquarters**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **1970** – **Ōshima Coal Mine closed**, and the shrine was merged with **Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/> * **October 1980** – **Rebuilt at the original site** with support from local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref> * **2018** – Relocated to **its current site**.
During the **Grand Festival in November**, **a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)** are performed.

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a **Shinto shrine** located on **Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture**. It is part of the **Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)**.
## 祭神 (Enshrined Deities) * **Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)** A major **mountain deity**, widely enshrined at **coal mines and gold mines** across Japan. Also known as **Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)**, meaning "God of Crossing," due to the **location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island**. As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also **worshiped as a sea deity** and is revered by **shipbuilding companies and maritime industries**.
## 歴史 (History) The **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island** was originally founded as a **guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine**. The shrine was established through an **enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was **merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**, where **Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi**, is enshrined. However, when **Ōshima Shipyard** was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings** worked together to **rebuild the shrine**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
### Key Events * **November 29, 1930** – Enshrined from **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima** and established as a **guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **December 6, 1936** – Relocated to **Ōshima Island** following the transfer of the **coal mine headquarters**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **1970** – **Ōshima Coal Mine closed**, and the shrine was merged with **Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/> * **October 1980** – **Rebuilt at the original site** with support from local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref> * **2018** – Relocated to **its current site**.
During the **Grand Festival in November**, **a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)** are performed.

### Key Events * **November 29, 1930** – Enshrined from **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima** and established as a **guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **December 6, 1936** – Relocated to **Ōshima Island** following the transfer of the **coal mine headquarters**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **1970** – **Ōshima Coal Mine closed**, and the shrine was merged with **Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/> * **October 1980** – **Rebuilt at the original site** with support from local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref> * **2018** – Relocated to **its current site**.
During the **Grand Festival in November**, **a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)** are performed.

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a **Shinto shrine** located on **Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture**. It is part of the **Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)**.
## 祭神 (Enshrined Deities) * **Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)** A major **mountain deity**, widely enshrined at **coal mines and gold mines** across Japan. Also known as **Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)**, meaning "God of Crossing," due to the **location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island**. As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also **worshiped as a sea deity** and is revered by **shipbuilding companies and maritime industries**.
## 歴史 (History) The **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island** was originally founded as a **guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine**. The shrine was established through an **enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was **merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**, where **Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi**, is enshrined. However, when **Ōshima Shipyard** was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings** worked together to **rebuild the shrine**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
### Key Events * **November 29, 1930** – Enshrined from **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima** and established as a **guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **December 6, 1936** – Relocated to **Ōshima Island** following the transfer of the **coal mine headquarters**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **1970** – **Ōshima Coal Mine closed**, and the shrine was merged with **Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/> * **October 1980** – **Rebuilt at the original site** with support from local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref> * **2018** – Relocated to **its current site**.
During the **Grand Festival in November**, **a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)** are performed.

* * [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (disambiguation) forks]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ōyamazumi Shrine (Saikai)}}[[Category:qq]] {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Ōyamazumi Shrine | image = {{religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image募集中|cat=Shrines|cat2=Saikai, Nagasaki}} | location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Saikai, Nagasaki|ja|西海市}}, {{ill|Ōshima (Saikai, Nagasaki)|ja|大島}} | deities = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神}} | established= 1930 | main_hall = | alias = | pilgrimage = | annual_festival = First Sunday of November | ceremonies = }}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a **Shinto shrine** located on **Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture**. It is part of the **Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)**.
## 祭神 (Enshrined Deities) * **Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)** A major **mountain deity**, widely enshrined at **coal mines and gold mines** across Japan. Also known as **Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)**, meaning "God of Crossing," due to the **location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island**. As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also **worshiped as a sea deity** and is revered by **shipbuilding companies and maritime industries**.
## 歴史 (History) The **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island** was originally founded as a **guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine**. The shrine was established through an **enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was **merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**, where **Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi**, is enshrined. However, when **Ōshima Shipyard** was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings** worked together to **rebuild the shrine**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
### Key Events * **November 29, 1930** – Enshrined from **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima** and established as a **guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **December 6, 1936** – Relocated to **Ōshima Island** following the transfer of the **coal mine headquarters**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **1970** – **Ōshima Coal Mine closed**, and the shrine was merged with **Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/> * **October 1980** – **Rebuilt at the original site** with support from local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref> * **2018** – Relocated to **its current site**.
During the **Grand Festival in November**, **a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)** are performed.

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a **Shinto shrine** located on **Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture**. It is part of the **Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)**.
## 祭神 (Enshrined Deities) * **Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)** A major **mountain deity**, widely enshrined at **coal mines and gold mines** across Japan. Also known as **Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)**, meaning "God of Crossing," due to the **location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island**. As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also **worshiped as a sea deity** and is revered by **shipbuilding companies and maritime industries**.
## 歴史 (History) The **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island** was originally founded as a **guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine**. The shrine was established through an **enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was **merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**, where **Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi**, is enshrined. However, when **Ōshima Shipyard** was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings** worked together to **rebuild the shrine**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
### Key Events * **November 29, 1930** – Enshrined from **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima** and established as a **guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **December 6, 1936** – Relocated to **Ōshima Island** following the transfer of the **coal mine headquarters**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **1970** – **Ōshima Coal Mine closed**, and the shrine was merged with **Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/> * **October 1980** – **Rebuilt at the original site** with support from local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref> * **2018** – Relocated to **its current site**.
During the **Grand Festival in November**, **a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)** are performed.

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a **Shinto shrine** located on **Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture**. It is part of the **Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)**.
## 祭神 (Enshrined Deities) * **Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)** A major **mountain deity**, widely enshrined at **coal mines and gold mines** across Japan. Also known as **Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)**, meaning "God of Crossing," due to the **location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island**. As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also **worshiped as a sea deity** and is revered by **shipbuilding companies and maritime industries**.
## 歴史 (History) The **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island** was originally founded as a **guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine**. The shrine was established through an **enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was **merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**, where **Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi**, is enshrined. However, when **Ōshima Shipyard** was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings** worked together to **rebuild the shrine**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
### Key Events * **November 29, 1930** – Enshrined from **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima** and established as a **guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **December 6, 1936** – Relocated to **Ōshima Island** following the transfer of the **coal mine headquarters**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **1970** – **Ōshima Coal Mine closed**, and the shrine was merged with **Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/> * **October 1980** – **Rebuilt at the original site** with support from local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref> * **2018** – Relocated to **its current site**.
During the **Grand Festival in November**, **a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)** are performed.

## 歴史 (History) The **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island** was originally founded as a **guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine**. The shrine was established through an **enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was **merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**, where **Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi**, is enshrined. However, when **Ōshima Shipyard** was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings** worked together to **rebuild the shrine**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
=== Key Events * **November 29, 1930** – Enshrined from **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima** and established as a **guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **December 6, 1936** – Relocated to **Ōshima Island** following the transfer of the **coal mine headquarters**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **1970** – **Ōshima Coal Mine closed**, and the shrine was merged with **Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/> * **October 1980** – **Rebuilt at the original site** with support from local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref> * **2018** – Relocated to **its current site**.
During the **Grand Festival in November**, **a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)** are performed.

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a **Shinto shrine** located on **Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture**. It is part of the **Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)**.
== 祭神 (Enshrined Deities) * **Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)** A major **mountain deity**, widely enshrined at **coal mines and gold mines** across Japan. Also known as **Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)**, meaning "God of Crossing," due to the **location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island**. As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also **worshiped as a sea deity** and is revered by **shipbuilding companies and maritime industries**.
== 歴史 (History) The **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island** was originally founded as a **guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine**. The shrine was established through an **enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was **merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**, where **Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi**, is enshrined. However, when **Ōshima Shipyard** was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings** worked together to **rebuild the shrine**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
=== Key Events * **November 29, 1930** – Enshrined from **Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima** and established as a **guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **December 6, 1936** – Relocated to **Ōshima Island** following the transfer of the **coal mine headquarters**.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/> * **1970** – **Ōshima Coal Mine closed**, and the shrine was merged with **Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/> * **October 1980** – **Rebuilt at the original site** with support from local residents, **Ōshima Shipyard**, and **Mitsui Matsushima Holdings**.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref> * **2018** – Relocated to **its current site**.
During the **Grand Festival in November**, **a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)** are performed.

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located on '''Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It is part of the '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)'''.
== 祭神 (Enshrined Deities)
* '''Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)'''
A major '''mountain deity''', widely enshrined at '''coal mines and gold mines''' across Japan.
Also known as '''Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)''', meaning "God of Crossing," due to the '''location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island'''.
As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also '''worshiped as a sea deity''' and is revered by '''shipbuilding companies and maritime industries'''.
== 歴史 (History)
The '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island''' was originally founded as a '''guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine'''. The shrine was established through an '''enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was '''merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island''', where '''Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi''', is enshrined. However, when '''Ōshima Shipyard''' was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings''' worked together to '''rebuild the shrine'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
=== Key Events
* '''November 29, 1930''' – Enshrined from '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima''' and established as a '''guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''December 6, 1936''' – Relocated to '''Ōshima Island''' following the transfer of the '''coal mine headquarters'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''1970''' – '''Ōshima Coal Mine closed''', and the shrine was merged with '''Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
* '''October 1980''' – '''Rebuilt at the original site''' with support from local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref>
* '''2018''' – Relocated to '''its current site'''.
During the '''Grand Festival in November''', '''a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)''' are performed.

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located on '''Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It is part of the '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)'''.
== 祭神 (Enshrined Deities)
* '''Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)'''
A major '''mountain deity''', widely enshrined at '''coal mines and gold mines''' across Japan.
Also known as '''Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)''', meaning "God of Crossing," due to the '''location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island'''.
As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also '''worshiped as a sea deity''' and is revered by '''shipbuilding companies and maritime industries'''.
== 歴史 (History)
The '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island''' was originally founded as a '''guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine'''. The shrine was established through an '''enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was '''merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island''', where '''Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi''', is enshrined. However, when '''Ōshima Shipyard''' was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings''' worked together to '''rebuild the shrine'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
=== Key Events
* '''November 29, 1930''' – Enshrined from '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima''' and established as a '''guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''December 6, 1936''' – Relocated to '''Ōshima Island''' following the transfer of the '''coal mine headquarters'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''1970''' – '''Ōshima Coal Mine closed''', and the shrine was merged with '''Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
* '''October 1980''' – '''Rebuilt at the original site''' with support from local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref>
* '''2018''' – Relocated to '''its current site'''.
During the '''Grand Festival in November''', '''a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)''' are performed.

{{Infobox Shinto shrine
| name = Ōyamazumi Shrine
| image = |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image募集中|cat=Shrines|cat2=Saikai, Nagasaki|
| location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Saikai, Nagasaki|ja|西海市}}, {{ill|Ōshima (Saikai, Nagasaki)|ja|大島}}
| deities = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神}}
| established= 1930
| main_hall =
| alias =
| pilgrimage =
| annual_festival = First Sunday of November
| ceremonies =
}}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located on '''Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It is part of the '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)'''.
== 祭神 (Enshrined Deities) ==
* '''Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)'''
A major '''mountain deity''', widely enshrined at '''coal mines and gold mines''' across Japan.
Also known as '''Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)''', meaning "God of Crossing," due to the '''location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island'''.
As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also '''worshiped as a sea deity''' and is revered by '''shipbuilding companies and maritime industries'''.
== 歴史 (History) ==
The '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island''' was originally founded as a '''guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine'''. The shrine was established through an '''enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was '''merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island''', where '''Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi''', is enshrined. However, when '''Ōshima Shipyard''' was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings''' worked together to '''rebuild the shrine'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
=== Key Events ===
* '''November 29, 1930''' – Enshrined from '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima''' and established as a '''guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''December 6, 1936''' – Relocated to '''Ōshima Island''' following the transfer of the '''coal mine headquarters'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''1970''' – '''Ōshima Coal Mine closed''', and the shrine was merged with '''Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
* '''October 1980''' – '''Rebuilt at the original site''' with support from local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref>
* '''2018''' – Relocated to '''its current site'''.
During the '''Grand Festival in November''', '''a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)''' are performed.
== 出典 (References) ==

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located on '''Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It is part of the '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)'''.
== 祭神 (Enshrined Deities) ==
* '''Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)'''
A major '''mountain deity''', widely enshrined at '''coal mines and gold mines''' across Japan.
Also known as '''Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)''', meaning "God of Crossing," due to the '''location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island'''.
As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also '''worshiped as a sea deity''' and is revered by '''shipbuilding companies and maritime industries'''.
== 歴史 (History) ==
The '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island''' was originally founded as a '''guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine'''. The shrine was established through an '''enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was '''merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island''', where '''Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi''', is enshrined. However, when '''Ōshima Shipyard''' was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings''' worked together to '''rebuild the shrine'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
=== Key Events ===
* '''November 29, 1930''' – Enshrined from '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima''' and established as a '''guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''December 6, 1936''' – Relocated to '''Ōshima Island''' following the transfer of the '''coal mine headquarters'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''1970''' – '''Ōshima Coal Mine closed''', and the shrine was merged with '''Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
* '''October 1980''' – '''Rebuilt at the original site''' with support from local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref>
* '''2018''' – Relocated to '''its current site'''.
During the '''Grand Festival in November''', '''a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)''' are performed.

== 歴史 (History) ==
The '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island''' was originally founded as a '''guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine'''. The shrine was established through an '''enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was '''merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island''', where '''Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi''', is enshrined. However, when '''Ōshima Shipyard''' was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings''' worked together to '''rebuild the shrine'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
=== Key Events ===
* '''November 29, 1930''' – Enshrined from '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima''' and established as a '''guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''December 6, 1936''' – Relocated to '''Ōshima Island''' following the transfer of the '''coal mine headquarters'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''1970''' – '''Ōshima Coal Mine closed''', and the shrine was merged with '''Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
* '''October 1980''' – '''Rebuilt at the original site''' with support from local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref>
* '''2018''' – Relocated to '''its current site'''.
During the '''Grand Festival in November''', '''a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)''' are performed.

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located on '''Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It is part of the '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)'''.
== 祭神 (Enshrined Deities) ==
* '''Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)'''
A major '''mountain deity''', widely enshrined at '''coal mines and gold mines''' across Japan.
Also known as '''Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)''', meaning "God of Crossing," due to the '''location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island'''.
As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also '''worshiped as a sea deity''' and is revered by '''shipbuilding companies and maritime industries'''.
== 歴史 (History) ==
The '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island''' was originally founded as a '''guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine'''. The shrine was established through an '''enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was '''merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island''', where '''Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi''', is enshrined. However, when '''Ōshima Shipyard''' was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings''' worked together to '''rebuild the shrine'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
=== Key Events ===
* '''November 29, 1930''' – Enshrined from '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima''' and established as a '''guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''December 6, 1936''' – Relocated to '''Ōshima Island''' following the transfer of the '''coal mine headquarters'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''1970''' – '''Ōshima Coal Mine closed''', and the shrine was merged with '''Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
* '''October 1980''' – '''Rebuilt at the original site''' with support from local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref>
* '''2018''' – Relocated to '''its current site'''.
During the '''Grand Festival in November''', '''a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)''' are performed.

{{Infobox Shinto shrine
| name = Ōyamazumi Shrine
| image = |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image募集中|cat=Shrines|cat2=Saikai, Nagasaki|
| location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Saikai, Nagasaki|ja|西海市}}, {{ill|Ōshima (Saikai, Nagasaki)|ja|大島}}
| deities = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神}}
| established= 1930
| main_hall =
| alias =
| pilgrimage =
| annual_festival = First Sunday of November
| ceremonies =
}}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located on '''Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It is part of the '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)'''.
== 祭神 (Enshrined Deities) ==
* '''Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)'''
A major '''mountain deity''', widely enshrined at '''coal mines and gold mines''' across Japan.
Also known as '''Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)''', meaning "God of Crossing," due to the '''location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island'''.
As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also '''worshiped as a sea deity''' and is revered by '''shipbuilding companies and maritime industries'''.
== 歴史 (History) ==
The '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island''' was originally founded as a '''guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine'''. The shrine was established through an '''enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was '''merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island''', where '''Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi''', is enshrined. However, when '''Ōshima Shipyard''' was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings''' worked together to '''rebuild the shrine'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
=== Key Events ===
* '''November 29, 1930''' – Enshrined from '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima''' and established as a '''guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''December 6, 1936''' – Relocated to '''Ōshima Island''' following the transfer of the '''coal mine headquarters'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''1970''' – '''Ōshima Coal Mine closed''', and the shrine was merged with '''Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
* '''October 1980''' – '''Rebuilt at the original site''' with support from local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref>
* '''2018''' – Relocated to '''its current site'''.
During the '''Grand Festival in November''', '''a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)''' are performed.
== 出典 (References) ==

| deities = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神}}

| main_hall =

| annual_festival = First Sunday of November

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located on '''Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It is part of the '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)'''.
== 祭神 (Enshrined Deities) ==
* '''Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)'''
A major '''mountain deity''', widely enshrined at '''coal mines and gold mines''' across Japan.
Also known as '''Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)''', meaning "God of Crossing," due to the '''location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island'''.
As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also '''worshiped as a sea deity''' and is revered by '''shipbuilding companies and maritime industries'''.
== 歴史 (History) ==
The '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island''' was originally founded as a '''guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine'''. The shrine was established through an '''enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was '''merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island''', where '''Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi''', is enshrined. However, when '''Ōshima Shipyard''' was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings''' worked together to '''rebuild the shrine'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
=== Key Events ===
* '''November 29, 1930''' – Enshrined from '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima''' and established as a '''guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''December 6, 1936''' – Relocated to '''Ōshima Island''' following the transfer of the '''coal mine headquarters'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''1970''' – '''Ōshima Coal Mine closed''', and the shrine was merged with '''Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
* '''October 1980''' – '''Rebuilt at the original site''' with support from local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref>
* '''2018''' – Relocated to '''its current site'''.
During the '''Grand Festival in November''', '''a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)''' are performed.

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located on '''Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It is part of the '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)'''.
== 祭神 (Enshrined Deities) ==
* '''Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)'''
A major '''mountain deity''', widely enshrined at '''coal mines and gold mines''' across Japan.
Also known as '''Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)''', meaning "God of Crossing," due to the '''location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island'''.
As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also '''worshiped as a sea deity''' and is revered by '''shipbuilding companies and maritime industries'''.
== 歴史 (History) ==
The '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island''' was originally founded as a '''guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine'''. The shrine was established through an '''enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was '''merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island''', where '''Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi''', is enshrined. However, when '''Ōshima Shipyard''' was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings''' worked together to '''rebuild the shrine'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
=== Key Events ===
* '''November 29, 1930''' – Enshrined from '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima''' and established as a '''guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''December 6, 1936''' – Relocated to '''Ōshima Island''' following the transfer of the '''coal mine headquarters'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''1970''' – '''Ōshima Coal Mine closed''', and the shrine was merged with '''Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
* '''October 1980''' – '''Rebuilt at the original site''' with support from local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref>
* '''2018''' – Relocated to '''its current site'''.
During the '''Grand Festival in November''', '''a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)''' are performed.

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located on '''Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It is part of the '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)'''.
== 祭神 (Enshrined Deities) ==
* '''Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)'''
A major '''mountain deity''', widely enshrined at '''coal mines and gold mines''' across Japan.
Also known as '''Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)''', meaning "God of Crossing," due to the '''location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island'''.
As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also '''worshiped as a sea deity''' and is revered by '''shipbuilding companies and maritime industries'''.
== 歴史 (History) ==
The '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island''' was originally founded as a '''guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine'''. The shrine was established through an '''enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was '''merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island''', where '''Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi''', is enshrined. However, when '''Ōshima Shipyard''' was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings''' worked together to '''rebuild the shrine'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
=== Key Events ===
* '''November 29, 1930''' – Enshrined from '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima''' and established as a '''guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''December 6, 1936''' – Relocated to '''Ōshima Island''' following the transfer of the '''coal mine headquarters'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''1970''' – '''Ōshima Coal Mine closed''', and the shrine was merged with '''Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
* '''October 1980''' – '''Rebuilt at the original site''' with support from local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref>
* '''2018''' – Relocated to '''its current site'''.
During the '''Grand Festival in November''', '''a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)''' are performed.

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located on '''Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It is part of the '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)'''.
== 祭神 (Enshrined Deities) ==
* '''Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)'''
A major '''mountain deity''', widely enshrined at '''coal mines and gold mines''' across Japan.
Also known as '''Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)''', meaning "God of Crossing," due to the '''location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island'''.
As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also '''worshiped as a sea deity''' and is revered by '''shipbuilding companies and maritime industries'''.
== 歴史 (History) ==
The '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island''' was originally founded as a '''guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine'''. The shrine was established through an '''enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was '''merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island''', where '''Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi''', is enshrined. However, when '''Ōshima Shipyard''' was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings''' worked together to '''rebuild the shrine'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
=== Key Events ===
* '''November 29, 1930''' – Enshrined from '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima''' and established as a '''guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''December 6, 1936''' – Relocated to '''Ōshima Island''' following the transfer of the '''coal mine headquarters'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''1970''' – '''Ōshima Coal Mine closed''', and the shrine was merged with '''Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
* '''October 1980''' – '''Rebuilt at the original site''' with support from local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref>
* '''2018''' – Relocated to '''its current site'''.
During the '''Grand Festival in November''', '''a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)''' are performed.

{{Infobox Shinto shrine
| name = Ōyamazumi Shrine
| image = |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image募集中|cat=Shrines|cat2=Saikai, Nagasaki|
| location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Saikai, Nagasaki|ja|西海市}}, {{ill|Ōshima (Saikai, Nagasaki)|ja|大島}}
| deities = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神}}
| established= 1930
| main_hall =
| alias =

| annual_festival = First Sunday of November
| ceremonies =
}}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located on '''Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It is part of the '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)'''.
== 祭神 (Enshrined Deities) ==
* '''Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)'''
A major '''mountain deity''', widely enshrined at '''coal mines and gold mines''' across Japan.
Also known as '''Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)''', meaning "God of Crossing," due to the '''location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island'''.
As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also '''worshiped as a sea deity''' and is revered by '''shipbuilding companies and maritime industries'''.
== 歴史 (History) ==
The '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island''' was originally founded as a '''guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine'''. The shrine was established through an '''enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was '''merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island''', where '''Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi''', is enshrined. However, when '''Ōshima Shipyard''' was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings''' worked together to '''rebuild the shrine'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
=== Key Events ===
* '''November 29, 1930''' – Enshrined from '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima''' and established as a '''guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''December 6, 1936''' – Relocated to '''Ōshima Island''' following the transfer of the '''coal mine headquarters'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''1970''' – '''Ōshima Coal Mine closed''', and the shrine was merged with '''Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
* '''October 1980''' – '''Rebuilt at the original site''' with support from local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref>
* '''2018''' – Relocated to '''its current site'''.
During the '''Grand Festival in November''', '''a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)''' are performed.

| name = Ōyamazumi Shrine
| image = |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|image募集中|cat=Shrines|cat2=Saikai, Nagasaki|
| location = {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}, {{ill|Saikai, Nagasaki|ja|西海市}}, {{ill|Ōshima (Saikai, Nagasaki)|ja|大島}}
| deities = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山積神}}
| established= 1930
| main_hall =
| alias =
| pilgrimage =
| annual_festival = First Sunday of November
| ceremonies =
}}
'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located on '''Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It is part of the '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)'''.
== 祭神 (Enshrined Deities) ==
* '''Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)'''
A major '''mountain deity''', widely enshrined at '''coal mines and gold mines''' across Japan.
Also known as '''Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)''', meaning "God of Crossing," due to the '''location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island'''.
As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also '''worshiped as a sea deity''' and is revered by '''shipbuilding companies and maritime industries'''.
== 歴史 (History) ==
The '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island''' was originally founded as a '''guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine'''. The shrine was established through an '''enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was '''merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island''', where '''Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi''', is enshrined. However, when '''Ōshima Shipyard''' was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings''' worked together to '''rebuild the shrine'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
=== Key Events ===
* '''November 29, 1930''' – Enshrined from '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima''' and established as a '''guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''December 6, 1936''' – Relocated to '''Ōshima Island''' following the transfer of the '''coal mine headquarters'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''1970''' – '''Ōshima Coal Mine closed''', and the shrine was merged with '''Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
* '''October 1980''' – '''Rebuilt at the original site''' with support from local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref>
* '''2018''' – Relocated to '''its current site'''.
During the '''Grand Festival in November''', '''a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)''' are performed.

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located on '''Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It is part of the '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)'''.
== 祭神 (Enshrined Deities) ==
* '''Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)'''
A major '''mountain deity''', widely enshrined at '''coal mines and gold mines''' across Japan.
Also known as '''Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)''', meaning "God of Crossing," due to the '''location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island'''.
As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also '''worshiped as a sea deity''' and is revered by '''shipbuilding companies and maritime industries'''.
== 歴史 (History) ==
The '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island''' was originally founded as a '''guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine'''. The shrine was established through an '''enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was '''merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island''', where '''Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi''', is enshrined. However, when '''Ōshima Shipyard''' was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings''' worked together to '''rebuild the shrine'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
=== Key Events ===
* '''November 29, 1930''' – Enshrined from '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima''' and established as a '''guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''December 6, 1936''' – Relocated to '''Ōshima Island''' following the transfer of the '''coal mine headquarters'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''1970''' – '''Ōshima Coal Mine closed''', and the shrine was merged with '''Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
* '''October 1980''' – '''Rebuilt at the original site''' with support from local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref>
* '''2018''' – Relocated to '''its current site'''.
During the '''Grand Festival in November''', '''a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)''' are performed.

'''Ōyamazumi Shrine''' (大山祇神社) is a '''Shinto shrine''' located on '''Ōshima Island in Saikai, Nagasaki Prefecture'''. It is part of the '''Mishima-Ōyamazumi faith (三島・大山祇信仰)'''.
== 祭神 (Enshrined Deities) ==
* '''Ōyamatsumi (大山積神)'''
A major '''mountain deity''', widely enshrined at '''coal mines and gold mines''' across Japan.
Also known as '''Watashi no Kami (和多志大神)''', meaning "God of Crossing," due to the '''location of the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island'''.
As a result, Ōyamatsumi is also '''worshiped as a sea deity''' and is revered by '''shipbuilding companies and maritime industries'''.
== 歴史 (History) ==
The '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōshima Island''' was originally founded as a '''guardian deity of Matsushima Coal Mine and Ōshima Coal Mine'''. The shrine was established through an '''enshrinement (勧請) from the main Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima Island, Ehime Prefecture'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi">[https://catalog.lib.kyushu-u.ac.jp/opac_download_md/26289/p213.pdf 全国鉱山と大山祇神社(第一輯)] (Ōyamazumi Shrine Office)</ref>
Following the closure of the mines, the shrine was '''merged with Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island''', where '''Konohanasakuya-hime (木花之佐久夜毘売), the daughter of Ōyamatsumi''', is enshrined. However, when '''Ōshima Shipyard''' was later established as part of industrial development, local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings''' worked together to '''rebuild the shrine'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
=== Key Events ===
* '''November 29, 1930''' – Enshrined from '''Ōyamazumi Shrine on Ōmishima''' and established as a '''guardian shrine of Matsushima Coal Mine'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''December 6, 1936''' – Relocated to '''Ōshima Island''' following the transfer of the '''coal mine headquarters'''.<ref name="Kōzan-Ōyamazumi"/>
* '''1970''' – '''Ōshima Coal Mine closed''', and the shrine was merged with '''Asama Shrine on Kakiura Island'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun"/>
* '''October 1980''' – '''Rebuilt at the original site''' with support from local residents, '''Ōshima Shipyard''', and '''Mitsui Matsushima Holdings'''.<ref name="NagasakiShimbun">[https://www.nagasaki-np.co.jp/kijis/?kijiid=563891249918166113 "Ōsaki High School Baseball Team Carries the Mikoshi – Remembering the Coal Mining Era"] (Nagasaki Shimbun)</ref>
* '''2018''' – Relocated to '''its current site'''.
During the '''Grand Festival in November''', '''a mikoshi procession and the sacred dance "Urayasu-no-Mai" (浦安の舞)''' are performed.
